title: Earthquake trapped-person signal routine
summary: A worst-case routine for saving strength while sending patterned signals and preserving rescue information.
content: Earthquake trapped-person signal routine is designed for conditions where normal services, internet, transport, or outside help may be delayed. The practical goal is saving strength while sending patterned signals and preserving rescue information. Keep the routine visible, assign one calm recorder, protect vulnerable people first, and change the plan only when conditions or official instructions clearly change.
materials: Notebook or board. Context: “Earthquake trapped-person signal routine”.
Marker or pencil. Context: “Earthquake trapped-person signal routine”.
Tape or labels. Context: “Earthquake trapped-person signal routine”.
Local map or contact list — for «Earthquake trapped-person signal routine»
steps: Write the exact trigger for starting earthquake trapped-person signal routine.
List people, supplies, hazards, and safe locations before action starts. Apply it specifically within the “Earthquake trapped-person signal routine” procedure.
Use the routine to focus on saving strength while sending patterned signals and preserving rescue information.
Check vulnerable people first and document unmet needs. Apply it specifically within the “Earthquake trapped-person signal routine” procedure.
Review the plan at fixed times or when official instructions change. Apply it specifically within the “Earthquake trapped-person signal routine” procedure.
warnings: Follow local authorities and qualified responders whenever they are available. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Earthquake trapped-person signal routine”.
Do not improvise with weapons, explosives, dangerous chemicals, medical dosing, or unsafe building entry. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Earthquake trapped-person signal routine”.

Do not let curiosity, filming, rumors, or pride replace distance, shelter, evacuation, hygiene, and records. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Earthquake trapped-person signal routine”.
common_mistakes: Starting too late because the first signs were ignored while carrying out “Earthquake trapped-person signal routine”
Letting many people make conflicting decisions at the same time while carrying out “Earthquake trapped-person signal routine”
Using memory instead of a visible written record while carrying out “Earthquake trapped-person signal routine”
alternatives: Run a smaller version of Earthquake trapped-person signal routine with one responsible person, one helper, and one visible paper note.
Use a safer fallback location or method for Earthquake trapped-person signal routine if the first choice becomes wet, crowded, contaminated, dark, unstable, or unsafe.
Pause Earthquake trapped-person signal routine and verify the written record with a second person before moving scarce supplies.
failure_signs: People cannot explain who is responsible for Earthquake trapped-person signal routine or when the next check happens.
Supplies connected to Earthquake trapped-person signal routine are unlabelled, damp, contaminated, damaged, missing, or being used for the wrong purpose.

The routine ignores shelter safety, weather exposure, ventilation, dry clothing, and exit routes and starts creating new risk instead of reducing existing risk during “Earthquake trapped-person signal routine”
when_not_to_use: Do not use Earthquake trapped-person signal routine as a reason to delay evacuation, emergency calls, medical care, fire response, or official local instructions.
Do not use Earthquake trapped-person signal routine when the location is structurally unsafe, chemically suspicious, smoky, flooded, violent, or otherwise immediately dangerous.
Do not improvise hazardous repairs, dosing, chemical handling, weapon use, or electrical/gas work under the label of Earthquake trapped-person signal routine.
short_term: For the first hours of Earthquake trapped-person signal routine, keep the action small, visible, and reversible: write the goal, assign one owner, protect vulnerable people, separate safe from unsafe supplies, and set a review time.
long_term: If Earthquake trapped-person signal routine remains necessary for days or weeks, rotate roles, preserve written lessons, replace fragile supplies, and train another person so the group does not depend on one expert.
if_method_fails: If Earthquake trapped-person signal routine fails, stop escalation, move to the safest fallback, record what failed, protect people first, and restart only with a simpler written plan.
environment_notes: Adapt Earthquake trapped-person signal routine to the real environment: check shelter safety, weather exposure, ventilation, dry clothing, and exit routes, local weather, building condition, water access, light, noise, animals, neighbours, and safe exits before repeating the routine.
